text.title: Spec Writing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Spec Writing. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Spec fits one page (≤700 words core). Longer = split.
rule.rationale: Spec fits one page (≤700 words core). Longer = split.

rule.statement: Spec lists ≥2 explicit non-goals; absent non-goals → scope creep.
rule.rationale: Spec lists ≥2 explicit non-goals; absent non-goals → scope creep.

rule.statement: Acceptance criteria are testable binary checks (Given/When/Then or rubric items).
rule.rationale: Acceptance criteria are testable binary checks (Given/When/Then or rubric items).

rule.statement: Open questions section exists, lists ≥1 question or 'none'.
rule.rationale: Open questions section exists, lists ≥1 question or 'none'.

rule.statement: Spec carries author + ≥1 reviewer signature before engineering starts.
rule.rationale: Spec carries author + ≥1 reviewer signature before engineering starts.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-spec-writing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
